verdict_reason: The most important philosophical pitfall of this verse is taking it as anti-Vedic. The engineering layer explicitly avoids that pitfall: 'Krishna does not reject the kāmya-karma section of scripture; he rejects the reading that treats that section as the totality.' Both Shankara's reading (specifically the panegyric/fruits-of-action portions are being criticised) and Ramanuja's reading (attachment-driven partial reading is criticised; the texts remain authoritative) are preserved without flattening. The transliterated terms (avipaścit, puṣpitā vāc, veda-vāda-rata, kāmya-karma) are kept load-bearing in the engineering text, not erased. The 'na anyad asti' clause is correctly read in the disagreements entry as 'a consequence of attachment, not a metaphysical claim'. PASS for both 7.4 and 6.8.
